market_context: Markets opened strongly higher despite ongoing Iran-Strait of Hormuz crisis with SPY up 1.15%, QQQ leading at +1.20%, as oil prices dropped 4% on hopes of US coalition to escort ships. Nvidia GTC conference beginning today is driving tech optimism with Jensen Huang's keynote at 11am PT expected to showcase AI breakthroughs.

predictions.reasoning: QQQ trading at strong premarket levels (+1.20% to 22,369) with Nvidia GTC conference driving tech optimism. Jensen Huang's keynote at 11am PT expected to showcase Vera Rubin architecture and inference breakthroughs, historically catalyzing semiconductor sector rallies.

SPY opened +1.15% as oil prices dropped 4% to $94.88 after Treasury Secretary Bessent announced allowing Iranian tankers passage. Market relief from geopolitical tensions and falling Treasury yields (10Y down 5.7 bps) supporting risk assets.

NVDA up 1.1% premarket ahead of GTC keynote. Wall Street consensus $273 price target (+52% upside) with 38/39 analysts rating Strong Buy. Historical GTC events drive outperformance vs semiconductor indices, and leaked expectations of Feynman architecture reveals.

Energy sector under pressure as oil drops 4% on US coalition hopes to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Materials companies like Mosaic (MOS) already down 3.79% in early trading, indicating sector rotation away from energy amid geopolitical relief.

Russell 2000 up 1.61% in early trading, reversing last week's losses. Small caps benefiting from falling oil prices and declining Treasury yields (10Y down 5.7 bps to 4.228%), creating favorable environment for domestic-focused companies.
